title: Bridge Essay: Shared mechanisms underlie mental imagery and motor planning.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Shared, mechanisms, underlie, mental, imagery.

**Shared mechanisms underlie mental imagery and motor planning.** — Sci Rep. PMID: 35194088. DOI: 10.1038/s41598-022-06800-9. Many studies have associated mental imagery with motor control mechanisms by showing mutually active brain areas and functions, as well as similar temporal patterns of imagining and executing the same motor actions. One of the main conjectured mutual mechanisms is the Cerebellar forward-model, commonly believed to generate sensory predictions as part of both motor control and mental imagery processes.
